# Freelance Tools — Free Offline Tools & Guides for Freelancers

> Free, single-file, offline-first tools for freelancers: invoicing, time tracking, CRM,
> expenses, contracts and quotes. No login, no subscription, your data stays in your browser.
> Paid Pro versions are one-time purchases on Gumroad. Site: https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/

## Free tools (run in the browser, nothing to install)
- [Free Invoice Generator](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/free-invoice-generator/): Offline single-file invoice & quote maker
- [Free Time Tracker](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/free-time-tracker/): Billable-hours timesheet, runs in your browser
- [Free Freelance CRM](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/free-freelance-crm/): Lead & client sales-pipeline tracker
- [Free Expense Tracker](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/free-expense-tracker/): Income, expenses & tax set-aside tracker
- [Free Freelance Contract](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/free-freelance-contract/): Proposal & service-contract maker

## Free calculators & utilities
- [Freelance Rate Calculator](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/freelance-rate-calculator/): Find/check your hourly rate from an income goal
- [Invoice Late-Fee Calculator](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/invoice-late-fee-calculator/): Compute overdue fees + reminder text
- [Hourly to Salary Calculator](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/hourly-to-salary-calculator/): Convert hourly <-> annual both ways
- [Project Quote Builder](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/project-quote-builder/): Line-item quote with discount/tax, print to PDF
- [Invoice Due-Date Calculator](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/invoice-due-date-calculator/): Net 7/15/30 due dates + early-pay discount
- [Freelance Tax Estimator](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/freelance-tax-estimator/): Quarterly/monthly set-aside estimate
- [Invoice Number Generator](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/invoice-number-generator/): Auto-incrementing invoice numbers
- [Freelance Project Checklist](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/freelance-project-checklist/): Pre-built 16-item project checklist

## Guides
- [How to Write a Freelance Invoice: Your Step-by-Step Guide](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-write-a-freelance-invoice/): Learn how to write a freelance invoice with the right fields, payment terms, tax details, and follow-up steps so clients can pay you promptly and reliably.
- [Offline Freelance Tools vs Cloud Apps: A Practical Guide](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/offline-vs-cloud-freelance-tools/): Compare offline freelance tools with cloud apps for privacy, data ownership, subscriptions, reliability, collaboration, and the long-term cost of each.
- [Time Tracking Methods for Freelancers: Timer, Manual, Blocks, and Pomodoro](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/time-tracking-methods-for-freelancers/): Compare time tracking methods for freelancers, including timers, manual logs, time-blocking, Pomodoro, billable vs non-billable time, invoices, and mistakes to avoid.
- [How to Organize Freelance Finances Without Making It Complicated](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-organize-freelance-finances/): Learn how to organize freelance finances with separate business money, income and expense tracking, tax set-asides, a monthly routine, and clean records.
- [How to Track Billable Hours as a Freelancer: Simple Guide](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-track-billable-hours/): Learn how to track billable hours, separate non-billable work, choose fair rounding rules, measure your effective rate, and create accurate client invoices.
- [How to Write a Freelance Contract (Plain-English Guide)](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-write-a-freelance-contract/): Learn how to write a freelance contract with clear scope, payment terms, revisions, ownership, cancellation, kill fee, signatures, and common mistakes to avoid.
- [How Much Should a Freelancer Save? Emergency Fund & Runway](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-much-should-a-freelancer-save/): How big a freelance emergency fund should be, how to calculate your runway in months, how to find your monthly break-even, and how to build a buffer against slow periods and late payments.
- [How Much Should Freelancers Set Aside for Taxes Each Year?](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-much-to-set-aside-for-taxes-freelance/): Learn how much to set aside for taxes freelance income may create, how the 25-30% habit works, and how to track expenses and estimated payments each year.
- [How to Ask for a Deposit as a Freelancer (Without Losing the Client)](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-ask-for-a-deposit-as-a-freelancer/): How much deposit to ask for, when to request it, the exact wording to use, and how to split a project into deposit and milestone payments so you are never working unpaid.
- [How to Follow Up on an Unpaid Invoice Without Sounding Pushy](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-follow-up-on-an-unpaid-invoice/): Learn how to follow up on an unpaid invoice with a calm sequence before the due date, day 1 overdue, day 7, day 14, late fees, records, and escalation.
- [How to Manage Multiple Freelance Clients Without Dropping Leads](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-to-manage-multiple-freelance-clients/): Learn how to manage multiple freelance clients with a lightweight CRM, clear pipeline stages, follow-up cadence, client prioritization, and simple weekly review.
- [Freelance Retainer Agreement: What to Include Before Monthly Work Starts](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-retainer-agreement/): Learn what a freelance retainer agreement is, how hours, deliverables, and access retainers work, what clauses to include, and how to price and bill one.
- [Freelance Sales Pipeline: A Simple System for More Leads](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-sales-pipeline/): Build a freelance sales pipeline that tracks leads, follow-ups, proposals, wins, and losses so promising client conversations do not disappear unnoticed.
- [How Much Do PayPal and Stripe Take From Freelancers? (2026)](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/how-much-do-paypal-stripe-take-from-freelancers/): A clear 2026 breakdown of PayPal, Stripe, Wise and Payoneer fees for freelancers, how to calculate what you actually keep, and how to price invoices so fees do not eat your rate.
- [How to Track Expenses as a Freelancer (Without a Spreadsheet)](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-expense-tracking/): Learn freelance expense tracking without a spreadsheet, including deductible categories, receipt habits, business separation, quarterly reviews, and tax set-aside planning.
- [Freelance Invoice Payment Terms Explained: Net 15, 30, 45 and How to Get Paid Faster](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-invoice-payment-terms/): Understand freelance invoice payment terms like Net 15, Net 30, deposits, 2/10 Net 30 discounts, late fees, follow-ups, and faster payment habits.
- [Free Freelance Invoice Template: What to Include and How to Send It](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-invoice-template/): Use this freelance invoice template guide to include the right fields, payment terms, line items, tax details, totals, and PDF sending process.
- [Estimate vs Quote vs Invoice: What Freelancers Should Send and When](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/estimate-vs-quote-vs-invoice/): Understand the difference between an estimate, quote, and invoice, when to send each document, what each should contain, and common freelance billing mistakes.
- [Freelance Contract Checklist: 10 Essential Clauses You Need](https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/blog/freelance-contract-checklist/): Use this freelance contract checklist to define scope, payment, revisions, ownership, cancellation, confidentiality, liability, signatures, and disputes.

## About
All tools are single HTML files that work offline with localStorage — no servers, no tracking.
Built for freelancers who want to own their data. Pro upgrades add unlimited records, CSV
export and a tool bundle. Full list & comparisons: https://btcson66-rgb.github.io/freelance-tools/best-free-freelance-tools/
